import yfinance as yf
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

import warnings

#---------------multi-asset universe — 40 instruments--------------------
tickers = ["SPY", "LMT", "NOC", "GD", "COP", "PSX", "MS", "BLK", "CME", "AMD", "AMAT", "CRM", "ABT", "TMO", "CL", "NEE", "EWG", "EWU", "EWQ", "EWI", "EWJ", "EWY", "EWT", "EWA", "EWZ", "SHY", "IEF", "TLT", "HYG", "EMB", "GLD", "SLV", "USO", "DBC", "UUP", "FXY", "FXE", "FXB", "RINF", "AIR.PA"]

start = "2016-01-01"

px = yf.download(tickers, start=start, auto_adjust=True, progress=False)["Close"]

# Basic cleaning
px = px.dropna()

    ("SPY","US Equity ETF","USA","Risk-on"),
    ("LMT","US Equity - Defense","USA","Risk-on"),
    ("NOC","US Equity - Defense","USA","Risk-on"),
    ("GD", "US Equity - Defense","USA","Risk-on"),

    ("AMD","US Equity - Tech","USA","Risk-on"),

vix_ret = vix_ret.dropna()

plt.xlabel("Date")

returns = np.log(px).diff().dropna()

print("Sample period :", px.index[0].date(), "to", px.index[-1].date())
print("Instruments :", len(px.columns))
print("Trading days :", len(px))

Trading days : 2545
In [8]:
#---------Target Variables----------------
spy_ret = returns['SPY']

y_dir = (spy_ret.shift(-1) > 0).astype(int) #Converts return momentum into a numbers 1 or 0

vol_proxy = spy_ret**2 #Vol = Squared returns
rv20 = vol_proxy.rolling(20).mean() #simple realised var proxy
threshold   = rv20.quantile(0.55)
y_vol       = (rv20.shift(-1) > threshold).astype(int)

rv_next = vol_proxy.shift(-1)
rv_next.name = "rv_next"

print("Direction (1=Up, 0=Down):", y_dir.value_counts())
print(f"\nFraction UP days: {y_dir.mean():.1%}")
print("\nVolatilty Regime (1=HighVol, 0=LowVol):", y_vol.value_counts())

Direction (1=Up, 0=Down): SPY
1    1406
0    1138
Name: count, dtype: int64

Fraction UP days: 55.3%

Volatilty Regime (1=HighVol, 0=LowVol): SPY
0    1408
1    1136
Name: count, dtype: int64
In [9]:
#---------Feature Engineering--------------
X = pd.DataFrame(index=returns.index)

#---SPY's own history----
X["ret_lag1"] = spy_ret.shift(1)
X["ret_lag5"] = spy_ret.rolling(5).sum().shift(1)   # last 5-day return, lagged
X["mom20"] = spy_ret.rolling(20).sum().shift(1)
X["mom60"] = spy_ret.rolling(60).sum().shift(1)
X["vol_20"] = spy_ret.rolling(20).std().shift(1)  # 20-day realized vol, lagged
X["vol_60"] = spy_ret.rolling(60).std().shift(1) 
X["SPY_ma10"] = spy_ret.rolling(10).mean().shift(1)    # Moving avg ratios
X["SPY_ma50"] = spy_ret.rolling(50).mean().shift(1)
X["SPY_ma_ratio_10_50"] = (X["SPY_ma10"] / X["SPY_ma50"]) - 1.0
X = X.drop(columns=["SPY_ma10", "SPY_ma50"])
X["ret_lag15"] = spy_ret.rolling(15).sum().shift(1)
X["mom10"] = spy_ret.rolling(10).sum().shift(1)
X["mom45"] = spy_ret.rolling(45).sum().shift(1)
X["vol_ratio"] = (X["vol_20"] / (X["vol_60"] + 1e-12))
X["vol_10"] = spy_ret.rolling(10).std().shift(1)
X["rv_lag1"] = (spy_ret**2).shift(1)
X["rv_lag5"] = (spy_ret**2).rolling(5).mean().shift(1)

# VIX features 
vix_level = vix.squeeze()
X["vix"] = vix_level.reindex(X.index).shift(1)
X["vix_chg1"] = np.log(vix_level).diff().shift(1).reindex(X.index)
X["vix_chg5"] = np.log(vix_level).diff(5).shift(1).reindex(X.index)
X["vix_z20"] = ((vix_level - vix_level.rolling(20).mean())/(vix_level.rolling(20).std() + 1e-12)).shift(1).reindex(X.index)
X["vix_sq"] = (vix_level**2).shift(1).reindex(X.index)
X["vix_vol_ratio"] = (vix_level / (spy_ret.rolling(20).std() * np.sqrt(252) * 100 + 1e-12)).shift(1).reindex(X.index)

# Bond / credit signals
X["TLT_ret"] = returns["TLT"].shift(1)
X["SHY_ret"] = returns["SHY"].shift(1)
X["term_spread"] = (returns["TLT"] - returns["SHY"]).shift(1)
X["credit_spread"] = (returns["HYG"] - returns["IEF"]).shift(1)
X["IEF_ret"] = returns["IEF"].shift(1)
X["HYG_ret"] = returns["HYG"].shift(1)
X["EMB_ret"] = returns["EMB"].shift(1)
X["bond_momentum"] = returns["TLT"].rolling(20).sum().shift(1)

# Commodity / FX signals
X["GLD_ret"] = returns["GLD"].shift(1)
X["DBC_ret"] = returns["DBC"].shift(1)
X["UUP_ret"] = returns["UUP"].shift(1)
X["FXY_ret"] = returns["FXY"].shift(1)
X["SLV_ret"] = returns["SLV"].shift(1)
X["GLD_mom20"] = returns["GLD"].rolling(20).sum().shift(1)
X["EWJ_ret"] = returns["EWJ"].shift(1)
X["EWZ_ret"] = returns["EWZ"].shift(1)
X["AIRPA_ret"] = returns["AIR.PA"].shift(1)
X["FXE_ret"] = returns["FXE"].shift(1)
X["UUP_mom20"] = returns["UUP"].rolling(20).sum().shift(1)
X["spy_tlt_corr"] = spy_ret.rolling(20).corr(returns["TLT"]).shift(1)

# Momentum behavior
vol_regime_raw = (X["vol_20"] > X["vol_20"].rolling(60).median()).astype(int)
X["mom_x_vol"] = X["mom20"] * vol_regime_raw
X["vol_accel"] = X["vol_20"] - X["vol_60"]
X["mom10_x_vol"] = X["mom10"] * vol_regime_raw
X["credit_x_vol"] = X["credit_spread"] * vol_regime_raw
X["vix_x_mom20"] = X["vix_z20"] * X["mom20"]
X["ret_lag1_sq"] = X["ret_lag1"]**2
X["vol_trend"] = (X["vol_20"] - X["vol_20"].shift(5))
X["spy_gld_corr"] = spy_ret.rolling(20).corr(returns["GLD"]).shift(1)
X["term_x_credit"] = X["term_spread"] * X["credit_spread"]

df_all = pd.concat([X, y_dir.rename("y_dir"), y_vol.rename("y_vol"), rv_next], axis=1).dropna()

X = df_all[X.columns]
y_dir = df_all["y_dir"]
y_vol = df_all["y_vol"]
rv_next = df_all["rv_next"]

print(f"Feature matrix: {X.shape[0]} days × {X.shape[1]} features")
print(f"Date range: {X.index[0].date()} → {X.index[-1].date()}")
print(f"\nFeatures: {list(X.columns)}")

Feature matrix: 2461 days × 49 features
Date range: 2016-04-04 → 2026-03-19

Features: ['ret_lag1', 'ret_lag5', 'mom20', 'mom60', 'vol_20', 'vol_60', 'SPY_ma_ratio_10_50', 'ret_lag15', 'mom10', 'mom45', 'vol_ratio', 'vol_10', 'rv_lag1', 'rv_lag5', 'vix', 'vix_chg1', 'vix_chg5', 'vix_z20', 'vix_sq', 'vix_vol_ratio', 'TLT_ret', 'SHY_ret', 'term_spread', 'credit_spread', 'IEF_ret', 'HYG_ret', 'EMB_ret', 'bond_momentum', 'GLD_ret', 'DBC_ret', 'UUP_ret', 'FXY_ret', 'SLV_ret', 'GLD_mom20', 'EWJ_ret', 'EWZ_ret', 'AIRPA_ret', 'FXE_ret', 'UUP_mom20', 'spy_tlt_corr', 'mom_x_vol', 'vol_accel', 'mom10_x_vol', 'credit_x_vol', 'vix_x_mom20', 'ret_lag1_sq', 'vol_trend', 'spy_gld_corr', 'term_x_credit']
In [10]:
#--------------Training, validation and testing-----------------

n = len(X)
train_end = int(n * 0.60)
val_end = int(n * 0.80)

X_train = X.iloc[:train_end]
X_val = X.iloc[train_end:val_end]
X_test = X.iloc[val_end:]

y_dir_train = y_dir.iloc[:train_end]
y_dir_val = y_dir.iloc[train_end:val_end]
y_dir_test = y_dir.iloc[val_end:]

y_vol_train = y_vol.iloc[:train_end]
y_vol_val = y_vol.iloc[train_end:val_end]
y_vol_test = y_vol.iloc[val_end:]

rv_train = rv_next.iloc[:train_end]
rv_val = rv_next.iloc[train_end:val_end]
rv_test = rv_next.iloc[val_end:]

# Re-define vol threshold using TRAIN only — prevents leakage
vol_thr = X_train["vol_20"].quantile(0.75)
y_vol_train = (X_train["vol_20"] > vol_thr).astype(int)
y_vol_val = (X_val["vol_20"]   > vol_thr).astype(int)
y_vol_test = (X_test["vol_20"]  > vol_thr).astype(int)

print("Split summary:")
print(f"TRAIN:{X_train.index[0].date()} → {X_train.index[-1].date()} ({len(X_train)} days)")
print(f"VAL:{X_val.index[0].date()} → {X_val.index[-1].date()}   ({len(X_val)} days)")
print(f"TEST:{X_test.index[0].date()} → {X_test.index[-1].date()}  ({len(X_test)} days)")
print(f"\nVol threshold (train only): {vol_thr:.6f}")
print(f"High-vol days in test: {y_vol_test.mean():.1%}")

Split summary:
TRAIN:2016-04-04 → 2022-03-16 (1476 days)
VAL:2022-03-17 → 2024-03-13   (492 days)
TEST:2024-03-14 → 2026-03-19  (493 days)

Vol threshold (train only): 0.010798
High-vol days in test: 15.2%
In [11]:
# Second normalization approach: cross-sectional z-scoring

from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

# Approach 1: Standard scaling (train-only fit) — used in all models
scaler_demo = StandardScaler()
X_std_sample = pd.DataFrame(
    scaler_demo.fit_transform(X_train),
    columns=X.columns, index=X_train.index
)

# Approach 2: Cross-sectional z-score — normalize across features on each date
X_cs = X.sub(X.mean(axis=1), axis=0).div(X.std(axis=1) + 1e-12, axis=0)

print("    Approach 1: Standard Scaling (train-only fit)")
print(X_std_sample.iloc[:3, :5].round(4))

print("\n    Approach 2: Cross-sectional z-score (by date)")
print(X_cs.iloc[:3, :5].round(4))

print("\nNote: Standard scaling is used for all models in this project.")
print("Cross-sectional z-scoring is shown for comparison — it normalizes")
print("each day's feature vector relative to itself, useful for ranking/panel approaches.")

    Approach 1: Standard Scaling (train-only fit)
            ret_lag1  ret_lag5   mom20   mom60  vol_20
Date                                                  
2016-04-04    0.5396    0.6660  0.7078 -0.0631 -0.4271

2016-04-06   -0.9211   -0.3164  0.2653 -0.0969 -0.3349

    Approach 2: Cross-sectional z-score (by date)

Date                                                  
2016-04-04   -0.1535   -0.1531 -0.1520 -0.1524 -0.1536

each day's feature vector relative to itself, useful for ranking/panel approaches.
In [12]:
# ----------sk imports + sharpe, maxdd, roc, auc, mse------------

from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score, accuracy_score, mean_squared_error

def sharpe(daily_ret, ann=252):
    daily_ret = np.asarray(daily_ret)
    mu = np.nanmean(daily_ret)
    sd = np.nanstd(daily_ret)
    return (mu/sd)*np.sqrt(ann) if sd > 0 else np.nan

def max_drawdown(cum):
    cum = np.asarray(cum)
    peak = np.maximum.accumulate(cum)
    dd = cum/peak - 1
    return np.min(dd)

def oos_reg_metrics(y_true, y_hat, y_train_mean):
    mse = mean_squared_error(y_true, y_hat)
    corr = np.corrcoef(y_true, y_hat)[0,1] if np.std(y_hat) > 1e-12 else np.nan
    mse_base = mean_squared_error(y_true, np.repeat(y_train_mean, len(y_true)))
    r2 = 1 - mse/(mse_base + 1e-12)
    return mse, corr, r2
In [13]:
#----------classification models-------------

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier, GradientBoostingClassifier
from sklearn.model_selection import TimeSeriesSplit
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay, roc_curve, brier_score_loss

def ts_cv_auc_for_logit(X, y, penalty, C_grid, n_splits=6):
    """
    Time-series CV AUC for logistic regression with given penalty.
    C = 1/lambda (bigger C => less regularization).
    """
    tscv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=n_splits)
    aucs = []

    for C in C_grid:
        solver = "saga"
        clf = Pipeline([
            ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
            ("logit", LogisticRegression(penalty=penalty, C=C, solver=solver,max_iter=10000))
        ])

        fold_aucs = []
        for tr_idx, va_idx in tscv.split(X):
            Xtr, Xva = X.iloc[tr_idx], X.iloc[va_idx]
            ytr, yva = y.iloc[tr_idx], y.iloc[va_idx]

            clf.fit(Xtr, ytr)
            p = clf.predict_proba(Xva)[:,1]
            fold_aucs.append(roc_auc_score(yva, p))

        aucs.append(np.mean(fold_aucs))
    return pd.Series(aucs, index=C_grid)

# Grid: wide range (log-scale)
C_grid = np.logspace(-3, 3, 20)

# RIDGE L2
ridge_cv = ts_cv_auc_for_logit(X_train, y_dir_train, penalty="l2", C_grid=C_grid)
best_C_ridge = float(ridge_cv.idxmax())
print(f"Best C (Ridge/L2): {best_C_ridge:.4f}  CV AUC: {float(ridge_cv.max()):.3f}")

# Lasso L1
lasso_cv = ts_cv_auc_for_logit(X_train, y_dir_train, penalty="l1", C_grid=C_grid)
best_C_lasso = float(lasso_cv.idxmax())
print(f"Best C (Lasso/L1): {best_C_lasso:.4f}  CV AUC: {float(lasso_cv.max()):.3f}")

# plot CV curves
plt.figure(figsize=(7,4))
plt.semilogx(C_grid, ridge_cv.values, marker="o", label="Ridge (L2)")
plt.semilogx(C_grid, lasso_cv.values, marker="o", label="Lasso (L1)")
plt.axvline(best_C_ridge, linestyle="--")
plt.axvline(best_C_lasso, linestyle="--")
plt.xlabel("C = 1/lambda (log scale)")
plt.ylabel("Time-series CV AUC")
plt.title("Tuning regularization via time-series CV")
plt.legend()
plt.show()

# Fit final models, plot confusion matrix, and evaluate Out-of-Sample
def plot_confusion(name, clf, Xtr, ytr, Xte, yte):
    """Fit model, plot confusion matrix, return probabilities."""
    clf.fit(Xtr, ytr)
    p = clf.predict_proba(Xte)[:, 1]
    pred = (p >= 0.5).astype(int)
    cm = confusion_matrix(yte, pred)
    ConfusionMatrixDisplay(cm, display_labels=["Down", "Up"]).plot(values_format="d")
    plt.title(f"Confusion Matrix (OOS) — {name}")
    plt.show()
    return p

# eval_clf also returns Brier Score and Brier Skill Score (BSS)
# BSS > 0 means the model beats the naive baseline — think of it like R² for classifiers
def eval_clf(name, y_true, proba):
    auc   = roc_auc_score(y_true, proba)
    acc   = accuracy_score(y_true, (proba >= 0.5).astype(int))
    brier = brier_score_loss(y_true, proba)
    brier_base = brier_score_loss(y_true, np.full(len(y_true), y_dir_train.mean()))
    bss   = 1 - brier / brier_base
    print(f"  {name:45s} | AUC: {auc:.3f} | Acc: {acc:.3f} | Brier: {brier:.4f} | BSS: {bss:.4f}")
    return {"Model": name, "AUC": round(auc,3), "Accuracy": round(acc,3),
            "Brier": round(brier,4), "BSS": round(bss,4)}

clf_results = []

p_base = np.full(len(y_dir_test), y_dir_train.mean())
clf_results.append(eval_clf("Baseline (training frequency)", y_dir_test, p_base))

# Logistic no penalty
logit_none = Pipeline([("scaler", StandardScaler()),("logit", LogisticRegression(penalty=None, solver="lbfgs", max_iter=10000))])
p_logit = plot_confusion("Logistic (no penalty)", logit_none, X_train, y_dir_train, X_test, y_dir_test)
clf_results.append(eval_clf("Logistic (no penalty)", y_dir_test, p_logit))

# Ridge
logit_ridge = Pipeline([("scaler", StandardScaler()),("logit", LogisticRegression(penalty="l2", C=best_C_ridge, solver="saga", max_iter=10000))])
p_ridge = plot_confusion(f"Ridge Logistic (C={best_C_ridge:.3f})", logit_ridge, X_train, y_dir_train, X_test, y_dir_test)
clf_results.append(eval_clf(f"Ridge Logistic (C={best_C_ridge:.3f})", y_dir_test, p_ridge))

# Lasso
logit_lasso = Pipeline([("scaler", StandardScaler()),("logit", LogisticRegression(penalty="l1", C=best_C_lasso, solver="saga", max_iter=10000))])
p_lasso = plot_confusion(f"Lasso Logistic (C={best_C_lasso:.3f})", logit_lasso, X_train, y_dir_train, X_test, y_dir_test)
clf_results.append(eval_clf(f"Lasso Logistic (C={best_C_lasso:.3f})", y_dir_test, p_lasso))

# Random Forest
rf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=400, max_depth=None, max_features="sqrt", random_state=42)
p_rf = plot_confusion("Random Forest (n=400)", rf, X_train, y_dir_train, X_test, y_dir_test)
clf_results.append(eval_clf("Random Forest (n=400)", y_dir_test, p_rf))

# Gradient Boosting
gb = GradientBoostingClassifier(n_estimators=200, learning_rate=0.05, max_depth=2, random_state=42)
p_gb = plot_confusion("Gradient Boosting (n=200, lr=0.05)", gb, X_train, y_dir_train, X_test, y_dir_test)
clf_results.append(eval_clf("Gradient Boosting (n=200, lr=0.05)", y_dir_test, p_gb))

# ROC curves
plt.figure(figsize=(7,5))
for name, p in [("None", p_logit),("Ridge", p_ridge),("Lasso", p_lasso),("RF", p_rf), ("GB", p_gb)]:
    fpr, tpr, _ = roc_curve(y_dir_test, p)
    plt.plot(fpr, tpr, label=f"{name} (AUC={roc_auc_score(y_dir_test,p):.3f})")

plt.plot([0,1],[0,1], linestyle="--")
plt.xlabel("FPR"); plt.ylabel("TPR")
plt.title("OOS ROC — Direction Classification")
plt.legend()
plt.show()

print("\n----Full Results Table-----")
print(pd.DataFrame(clf_results).sort_values("AUC", ascending=False).to_string(index=False))

# Tree hyperparameter grid
from itertools import product

tree_grid_results = []
depths  = [2, 4, None]
n_trees = [100, 300]
lr_gb   = [0.01, 0.05]

# RF grid — 6 combinations (3 depths × 2 n_trees)
for d, n in product(depths, n_trees):
    m = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=n, max_depth=d,max_features="sqrt", random_state=42)
    m.fit(X_train, y_dir_train)
    p = m.predict_proba(X_test)[:,1]
    auc = roc_auc_score(y_dir_test, p)
    tree_grid_results.append({"Model":"RF","depth":str(d),"n_est":n,"lr":"-","AUC":round(auc,3)})

# GB grid — 8 combinations (2 depths × 2 n_trees × 2 lr)
for d, n, lr in product([2,4], [100,200], lr_gb):
    m = GradientBoostingClassifier(n_estimators=n, learning_rate=lr, max_depth=d, random_state=42)
    m.fit(X_train, y_dir_train)
    p = m.predict_proba(X_test)[:,1]
    auc = roc_auc_score(y_dir_test, p)
    tree_grid_results.append({"Model":"GB","depth":str(d),"n_est":n,"lr":lr,"AUC":round(auc,3)})

print(f"\nTree hyperparameter grid — {len(tree_grid_results)} combinations")
print(pd.DataFrame(tree_grid_results).sort_values("AUC", ascending=False).to_string(index=False))

In [14]:
#--------regression models-------------
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression, Ridge, Lasso
from sklearn.model_selection import TimeSeriesSplit

def ts_cv_mse_reg(X, y, model_type, alpha_grid, n_splits=6):
    """
    Time-series CV MSE for Ridge or Lasso regression.
    alpha = lambda (bigger alpha => stronger shrinkage)
    """
    tscv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=n_splits)
    mses = []

    for a in alpha_grid:
        if model_type == "ridge":
            model = Ridge(alpha=a)
        elif model_type == "lasso":
            model = Lasso(alpha=a, max_iter=30000)

        pipe = Pipeline([("scaler", StandardScaler()), ("model", model)])

        fold_mses = []
        for tr_idx, va_idx in tscv.split(X):
            X_tr, X_va = X.iloc[tr_idx], X.iloc[va_idx]
            y_tr, y_va = y.iloc[tr_idx], y.iloc[va_idx]

            pipe.fit(X_tr, y_tr)
            fold_mses.append(mean_squared_error(y_va, pipe.predict(X_va)))

        mses.append(np.mean(fold_mses))

    return pd.Series(mses, index=alpha_grid)

# ------------------------------------------------------
# Choose alpha grid (wide, log-scale)
# ------------------------------------------------------
alpha_grid = np.logspace(-8, 2, 30)

ridge_cv_rv = ts_cv_mse_reg(X_train, rv_train, "ridge", alpha_grid)
lasso_cv_rv = ts_cv_mse_reg(X_train, rv_train, "lasso", alpha_grid)

best_a_ridge = float(ridge_cv_rv.idxmin())
best_a_lasso = float(lasso_cv_rv.idxmin())

print(f"Best alpha Ridge: {best_a_ridge:.2e}  CV MSE: {float(ridge_cv_rv.min()):.2e}")
print(f"Best alpha Lasso: {best_a_lasso:.2e}  CV MSE: {float(lasso_cv_rv.min()):.2e}")

plt.figure(figsize=(7,4))
plt.semilogx(alpha_grid, ridge_cv_rv.values, marker="o", label="Ridge")
plt.semilogx(alpha_grid, lasso_cv_rv.values, marker="o", label="Lasso")
plt.axvline(best_a_ridge, linestyle="--")
plt.axvline(best_a_lasso, linestyle="--")
plt.xlabel("alpha = lambda (log scale)")
plt.ylabel("Time-series CV MSE")
plt.title("Tuning shrinkage for RV regression (target = rv_next)")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.25)
plt.show()

ols_reg   = Pipeline([("scaler", StandardScaler()), ("model", LinearRegression())])
ridge_reg = Pipeline([("scaler", StandardScaler()), ("model", Ridge(alpha=best_a_ridge))])
lasso_reg = Pipeline([("scaler", StandardScaler()), ("model", Lasso(alpha=best_a_lasso, max_iter=30000))])

ols_reg.fit(X_train, rv_train)
ridge_reg.fit(X_train, rv_train)
lasso_reg.fit(X_train, rv_train)

# OOS forecast metrics
ybar = float(rv_train.mean())

print("\nVolatility Regression — TEST SET")
reg_results = []
for name, yhat in [
    ("Baseline (mean forecast)", np.repeat(ybar, len(rv_test))),
    ("OLS", ols_reg.predict(X_test)),
    (f"Ridge (α={best_a_ridge:.2e})", ridge_reg.predict(X_test)),
    (f"Lasso (α={best_a_lasso:.2e})", lasso_reg.predict(X_test)),
]:
    mse, corr, r2 = oos_reg_metrics(rv_test, yhat, ybar)
    print(f"  {name:35s} | MSE: {mse:.2e} | Corr(y,yhat): {corr:.3f} | OOS R2(vs mean): {r2:.3f}")
    reg_results.append({"Model": name, "MSE": round(mse,8),"Corr(y,yhat)": round(corr,3), "OOS R2(vs mean)": round(r2,3)})

print("\nFull Table")
print(pd.DataFrame(reg_results).sort_values("OOS R2(vs mean)", ascending=False).to_string(index=False))

# Tree-based regressors (required by assignment)
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor, GradientBoostingRegressor

rf_reg = RandomForestRegressor(n_estimators=300, max_depth=4, random_state=42)
gb_reg = GradientBoostingRegressor(n_estimators=200, learning_rate=0.05, max_depth=3, random_state=42)
rf_reg.fit(X_train, rv_train)
gb_reg.fit(X_train, rv_train)

print("\nTree-based Regressors — TEST SET")
for name, yhat in [
    ("Random Forest Regressor",     rf_reg.predict(X_test)),
    ("Gradient Boosting Regressor", gb_reg.predict(X_test)),
]:
    mse, corr, r2 = oos_reg_metrics(rv_test, yhat, ybar)
    print(f"  {name:35s} | MSE: {mse:.2e} | Corr: {corr:.3f} | OOS R²: {r2:.3f}")
    reg_results.append({"Model": name, "MSE": round(mse,8),"Corr(y,yhat)": round(corr,3), "OOS R2(vs mean)": round(r2,3)})

print("\nFull Regression Table (all models)")
print(pd.DataFrame(reg_results).sort_values("OOS R2(vs mean)", ascending=False).to_string(index=False))

In [15]:
#------------trading strats------------

def perf_stats(r, ann=252):
    r = pd.Series(r).dropna()
    cum     = (1+r).cumprod()
    ann_ret = cum.iloc[-1]**(ann/len(r)) - 1 if len(r) > 0 else np.nan
    ann_vol = r.std()*np.sqrt(ann)
    sharpe_ = (r.mean()/(r.std()+1e-12))*np.sqrt(ann)
    peak    = cum.cummax()
    maxdd   = (cum/peak - 1).min()
    return pd.Series({"AnnRet": ann_ret, "AnnVol": ann_vol, "Sharpe": sharpe_, "MaxDD": maxdd})

test_idx     = X_test.index
spy_ret_test = returns["SPY"].shift(-1).loc[test_idx]
shy_ret_test = returns["SHY"].shift(-1).loc[test_idx]
p_dir        = pd.Series(p_ridge, index=test_idx)

vol_clf = Pipeline([("scaler", StandardScaler()), ("logit", LogisticRegression(penalty="l2", C=best_C_ridge, solver="saga", max_iter=10000))])
vol_clf.fit(X_train, y_vol_train)
q_vol = pd.Series(vol_clf.predict_proba(X_test)[:,1], index=test_idx)

results = {}
results["Buy & Hold SPY"] = perf_stats(spy_ret_test)

# hard threshold +1/-1 with 1-day lag
signal_A1 = pd.Series(0.0, index=test_idx)
signal_A1[p_dir > 0.55] =  1.0
signal_A1[p_dir < 0.45] = -1.0
signal_A1 = signal_A1.shift(1).fillna(0)
results["A1: Hard threshold (0.55/0.45)"] = perf_stats(signal_A1 * spy_ret_test)

# confidence band, flat in middle
signal_A2 = pd.Series(0.0, index=test_idx)
signal_A2[p_dir > 0.60] =  1.0
signal_A2[p_dir < 0.40] = -1.0
signal_A2 = signal_A2.shift(1).fillna(0.0)
results["A2: Confidence band (0.60/0.40)"] = perf_stats(signal_A2 * spy_ret_test)

# smooth_weight sizing
sig_A3 = (p_dir - 0.5).clip(-0.5, 0.5) * 2
sig_A3 = sig_A3.shift(1).fillna(0)
results["A3: Smooth sizing"] = perf_stats(sig_A3 * spy_ret_test)

# vol-managed SPY/SHY switch
exposure = pd.Series(1.0, index=test_idx)
exposure[q_vol > 0.60] = 0.0
exposure = exposure.shift(1).fillna(1.0)
strat_B  = exposure * spy_ret_test + (1 - exposure) * shy_ret_test
results["B: Vol switch (SPY/SHY)"] = perf_stats(strat_B)

# Print comparison table
print("Strategy Comparison (TEST PERIOD)")
print(pd.DataFrame(results).T.to_string())

# cumulative return plot
plt.figure(figsize=(12,5))
(1 + spy_ret_test).cumprod().plot(label="Buy & Hold SPY", color="black", linewidth=2)
(1 + signal_A1 * spy_ret_test).cumprod().plot(label="A1: Hard threshold", linestyle="--")
(1 + signal_A2 * spy_ret_test).cumprod().plot(label="A2: Confidence band", linestyle="--")
(1 + sig_A3 * spy_ret_test).cumprod().plot(label="A3: Smooth sizing", linestyle="--")
(1 + strat_B).cumprod().plot(label="B: Vol switch", linestyle="-.", linewidth=2)
plt.title("Strategy Cumulative Returns — Test Period")
plt.ylabel("Growth of $1")
plt.legend(loc="upper center", bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.12), ncol=3, fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Sharpe + MaxDD summary
print("\nSharpe & MaxDD")
print(pd.DataFrame(results).T[["AnnRet","AnnVol","Sharpe","MaxDD"]].to_string())

# Turnover reporting (required by assignment)
print("\nTurnover (avg daily position change)")
for name, sig in [("A1 Hard threshold", signal_A1),
                   ("A2 Confidence band", signal_A2),
                   ("A3 Smooth sizing",   sig_A3),
                   ("B  Vol switch",      exposure)]:
    turnover = sig.diff().abs().mean()
    print(f"{name}: {turnover:.4f} avg daily position change")

#--------model interpretation & stability-----------
def fit_logit_and_get_coef(X, y, penalty, C):
    clf = Pipeline([
        ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
        ("logit", LogisticRegression(penalty=penalty, C=C, solver="saga", max_iter=20000))
    ])
    clf.fit(X, y)
    return pd.Series(clf.named_steps["logit"].coef_.ravel(), index=X.columns)

# build coefficient paths across C grid
C_path = np.logspace(-3, 3, 40)
ridge_path = pd.DataFrame(index=C_path, columns=X_train.columns, dtype=float)
lasso_path = pd.DataFrame(index=C_path, columns=X_train.columns, dtype=float)

for C in C_path:
    ridge_path.loc[C] = fit_logit_and_get_coef(X_train, y_dir_train, "l2", C).values
    lasso_path.loc[C] = fit_logit_and_get_coef(X_train, y_dir_train, "l1", C).values

# plot top-12 coefficient paths
top_k      = 12
ridge_top  = ridge_path.abs().max().sort_values(ascending=False).index[:top_k].tolist()
lasso_top  = lasso_path.abs().max().sort_values(ascending=False).index[:top_k].tolist()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 5))

ax = axes[0]
for col in ridge_top:
    ax.semilogx(ridge_path.index, ridge_path[col].values, linewidth=2, label=col)
ax.set_title("Ridge (L2) Logistic — Coefficient Path")
ax.set_xlabel("C = 1/lambda (log scale)")
ax.set_ylabel("Coefficient (standardized features)")
ax.axhline(0, linewidth=1); ax.grid(True, alpha=0.25)
ax.legend(fontsize=8, ncol=2)

ax = axes[1]
for col in lasso_top:
    ax.semilogx(lasso_path.index, lasso_path[col].values, linewidth=2, label=col)
ax.set_title("Lasso (L1) Logistic — Coefficient Path (sparsity)")
ax.set_xlabel("C = 1/lambda (log scale)")
ax.set_ylabel("Coefficient (standardized features)")
ax.axhline(0, linewidth=1); ax.grid(True, alpha=0.25)
ax.legend(fontsize=8, ncol=2)

plt.suptitle("Regularization Paths — Ridge vs Lasso", y=1.02, fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Lasso sparsity across C
nonzero_counts = (lasso_path.abs() > 1e-12).sum(axis=1)
plt.figure(figsize=(7,4))
plt.semilogx(nonzero_counts.index, nonzero_counts.values, marker="o")
plt.xlabel("C = 1/lambda (log scale)")
plt.ylabel("# Non-zero coefficients")
plt.title("Lasso sparsity: number of selected features vs regularization strength")
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.25)
plt.show()

# Lasso sparsity at best C
logit_lasso.fit(X_train, y_dir_train)
coef = logit_lasso.named_steps["logit"].coef_.ravel()
coef_s = pd.Series(coef, index=X_train.columns).sort_values(key=np.abs, ascending=False)
print(f"\nNon-zero Lasso coefficients: {int((coef_s != 0).sum())} out of {len(coef_s)} ──")
print(coef_s[coef_s != 0])

# stability selection bar chart
selection_matrix    = (lasso_path.abs() > 1e-12).astype(int)
selection_frequency = selection_matrix.mean(axis=0).sort_values(ascending=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10,5))
selection_frequency.head(20).sort_values().plot(kind="barh", color="steelblue")
plt.title("Stability Selection (Lasso)\nFeature persistence across regularization strengths")
plt.xlabel("Selection frequency (fraction of lambdas where feature is active)")
plt.grid(axis="x", alpha=0.3)
plt.show()

# RF feature importance
imp = pd.Series(rf.feature_importances_, index=X_train.columns).sort_values()
imp.plot.barh(figsize=(8,7), color="steelblue")
plt.title("Random Forest Feature Importance")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

#--------------scenario testing-----------------

scenario_results = []

# run_scenario helper
def run_scenario(sid, desc, C, penalty, thr_long, thr_short, cost_bps):
    m = Pipeline([
        ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
        ("logit", LogisticRegression(C=C, penalty=penalty, solver="saga", max_iter=10000))
    ])
    m.fit(X_train, y_dir_train)
    p   = pd.Series(m.predict_proba(X_test)[:,1], index=test_idx)
    auc = roc_auc_score(y_dir_test, p)

    # signal construction
    sig = pd.Series(0.0, index=test_idx)
    sig[p > thr_long]  =  1.0
    sig[p < thr_short] = -1.0
    sig = sig.shift(1).fillna(0.0)

    cost    = cost_bps / 10000
    net_ret = sig * spy_ret_test - sig.diff().abs() * cost
    sharpe_ = (net_ret.mean()/(net_ret.std()+1e-12))*np.sqrt(252)
    ann_ret = net_ret.mean() * 252

    scenario_results.append({
        "ID": sid, "Description": desc, "Penalty": penalty,
        "C": round(C,4), "Thr+": thr_long, "Thr-": thr_short,
        "Cost(bps)": cost_bps, "AUC": round(auc,3),
        "Ann.Ret": f"{ann_ret:.2%}", "Sharpe": round(sharpe_,2)
    })

# Group A — Baselines
run_scenario("S01","No penalty, thr=0.50",        1e6,         "l2", 0.50, 0.50,  0)
run_scenario("S02","Ridge best C, thr=0.50", best_C_ridge,     "l2", 0.50, 0.50,  0)
run_scenario("S03","Lasso best C, thr=0.50", best_C_lasso,     "l1", 0.50, 0.50,  0)

# Group B — Hyperparameter sweep
run_scenario("S04","Ridge C=0.001",              0.001,        "l2", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S05","Ridge C=0.01",               0.010,        "l2", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S06","Ridge C=0.1",                0.100,        "l2", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S07","Ridge C=1.0",                1.000,        "l2", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S08","Ridge C=10.0",              10.000,        "l2", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S09","Ridge C=100",              100.000,        "l2", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S10","Lasso C=0.001",              0.001,        "l1", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S11","Lasso C=100",              100.000,        "l1", 0.55, 0.45,  0)

# Group C — Threshold sensitivity
run_scenario("S12","Ridge best C, thr=0.50/0.50", best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.50, 0.50, 0)
run_scenario("S13","Ridge best C, thr=0.52/0.48", best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.52, 0.48, 0)
run_scenario("S14","Ridge best C, thr=0.55/0.45", best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.55, 0.45, 0)
run_scenario("S15","Ridge best C, thr=0.58/0.42", best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.58, 0.42, 0)
run_scenario("S16","Ridge best C, thr=0.60/0.40", best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.60, 0.40, 0)

# Group D — Transaction cost sensitivity
run_scenario("S17","Ridge best C, 0bps",  best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S18","Ridge best C, 5bps",  best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.55, 0.45,  5)
run_scenario("S19","Ridge best C, 10bps", best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.55, 0.45, 10)
run_scenario("S20","Ridge best C, 20bps", best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.55, 0.45, 20)
run_scenario("S21","Ridge best C, 50bps", best_C_ridge, "l2", 0.55, 0.45, 50)

# Group E — Lasso cost sensitivity
run_scenario("S22","Lasso best C, 0bps",          best_C_lasso, "l1", 0.55, 0.45,  0)
run_scenario("S23","Lasso best C, 5bps",          best_C_lasso, "l1", 0.55, 0.45,  5)
run_scenario("S24","Lasso best C, 20bps",         best_C_lasso, "l1", 0.55, 0.45, 20)
run_scenario("S25","Lasso best C, thr=0.60, 0bps",  best_C_lasso, "l1", 0.60, 0.40, 0)
run_scenario("S26","Lasso best C, thr=0.60, 20bps", best_C_lasso, "l1", 0.60, 0.40, 20)

print(f"{len(scenario_results)} scenarios completed\n")
print(pd.DataFrame(scenario_results).to_string(index=False))

# Stress Test on Crisis Windows 
crisis_windows = {
    "Early Test (2024 Q2)":   ("2024-04-01", "2024-09-30"),
    "Market Drop 2025 Q1":    ("2025-01-01", "2025-06-30"),
    "Late Test (2025 H2)":    ("2025-07-01", "2026-03-11"),
    "Full Test Period":        (str(X_test.index[0].date()), str(X_test.index[-1].date())),
}

strat_ret_full = signal_A1 * spy_ret_test

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 8))
axes = axes.flatten()

print(f"\n{'Window':<25} {'Strat Sharpe':>13} {'B&H Sharpe':>12} {'Strat MaxDD':>13} {'B&H MaxDD':>11}")
print("-" * 78)

for i, (label, (s, e)) in enumerate(crisis_windows.items()):
    mask  = (strat_ret_full.index >= s) & (strat_ret_full.index <= e)
    s_ret = strat_ret_full[mask]
    b_ret = spy_ret_test[mask]

    if len(s_ret) < 5:
        axes[i].set_title(f"{label}\n(no data in test period)")
        continue

    s_cum = (1 + s_ret).cumprod()
    b_cum = (1 + b_ret).cumprod()

    print(f"{label:<25} {sharpe(s_ret):>13.2f} {sharpe(b_ret):>12.2f}" f"{max_drawdown(s_cum):>13.2%} {max_drawdown(b_cum):>11.2%}")

    # drawdown-style cumulative plot
    s_cum.plot(ax=axes[i], label="Hard Switch", color="steelblue")
    b_cum.plot(ax=axes[i], label="Buy & Hold",  color="black", linestyle="--")
    axes[i].set_title(label)
    axes[i].legend(fontsize=8)
    axes[i].set_ylabel("Growth of $1")

# vol comparison
print(f"\nAnnualised vol (full test period)")
print(f"Hard Switch : {strat_ret_full.std()*np.sqrt(252):.2%}")
print(f"Buy & Hold    : {spy_ret_test.std()*np.sqrt(252):.2%}")

plt.suptitle("Stress Test — Hard Switch vs Buy & Hold", fontsize=13)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
